deep_dive.title: Physics-Informed Diffusion for Biomechanically Plausible 3D Sign Language Generation
deep_dive.abstract: Sign language production, which generates continuous 3D skeletal motion from spoken language input, must simultaneously satisfy two constraints: semantic fidelity, so that a deaf viewer can recognize the intended sequence of glosses, and biomechanical plausibility, so that the generated skeleton respects anatomical constraints. Existing approaches optimize semantic reconstruction through coordinate-based objectives that treat the skeleton as an unstructured vector, thus allowing for bone length drift, joint angle violations, and temporarily locked fingers. We introduce PIDiffSign, a physics-informed diffusion model for gloss-to-pose translation that incorporates anatomical constraints into both the architecture and training objective. The model uses a Transformer encoder-decoder, where the decoder is conditioned on the diffusion time step through adaptive zero-initialized layer normalization and cross-attends to gloss representations. A differentiable geometry module enforces bone length consistency and biologically valid joint angles throughout generation. Training combines anthropomorphic, kinematic, angular, and finger-joint constraints with a contrastive gloss-pose alignment loss and classifier-free guidance for semantically conditioned sampling. Experiments on the PHOENIX14T and CSL-Daily benchmarks show consistent improvements over a strong diffusion baseline in pose accuracy, joint-angle correctness, distributional realism, and back-translation quality. These results demonstrate that physics-informed diffusion improves both motion realism and semantic fidelity for sign language generation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: diffusion model for 3D sign language motion generation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于3D手语动作生成的扩散模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于3D手语动作生成的扩散模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于3D手语动作生成的扩散模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes PIDiffSign, a physics-informed diffusion model that generates biomechanically plausible 3D sign language motion by incorporating anatomical constraints into architecture and training.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有3D手语生成方法忽略骨骼生物力学约束，导致骨长漂移和关节角度违规。本文提出PIDiffSign，一种物理信息扩散模型，在架构和训练目标中融入解剖学约束。实验表明，该方法在保持语义保真度的同时显著提升生成骨骼的 biomechanical 合理性。为3D人体运动生成中融入物理约束提供了新思路。

deep_dive.title: ConFlow: Constraints-Guided Learning with Flow Matching for Motion Generation
deep_dive.abstract: In recent years Flow Matching has become a prominent method for generative modeling robot motion generation. In its generic form Flow Matching is an ODE-based neural sampler that is trained by regressing empirical flow fields associated with motion samples as data. However, in robot motion generation we often have additional constraints that might not be present in the collected data. The majority of current approaches train the flow on the available data and use inference-time guidance to enforce task-specific constraints. To address this mismatch, we propose \textbf{ConFlow}, a constraint-guided flow matching framework that incorporates constraint information directly into the training objective via differentiable barrier or cost functions. To address design specifications such as smoothness and boundary conditions, we propose replacing the standard Gaussian source distribution used in flow matching training with a conditional Gaussian Process. Our approach also uses infeasible demonstrations as negative supervision, improving constraint satisfaction without requiring additional expert data. Experiments on a two-robot navigation task demonstrate that ConFlow achieves lower collision rates and higher trajectory quality than standard flow matching baselines, with or without inference-time guidance. These results validate training-time constraint integration as an effective approach to closing the training--inference gap in generative motion models.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: flow matching for motion generation with constraints
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 流匹配在带约束的运动生成中的应用
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 流匹配在带约束的运动生成中的应用
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 流匹配在带约束的运动生成中的应用
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: ConFlow proposes constraint-guided flow matching that integrates task-specific constraints into training for motion generation, directly relevant to flow matching-based generative modeling.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: ConFlow提出约束引导的流匹配框架，将任务特定约束直接融入训练目标，用于运动生成。该方法聚焦于机器人运动，但其流匹配机制可迁移至3D人体运动生成，为后续研究提供了方法论基础。实验表明，该方法在满足约束的同时保持了生成质量。

deep_dive.title: MeanFlowNFT: Bringing Forward-Process RL to Average-Velocity Generators
deep_dive.abstract: MeanFlow generators achieve fast few-step sampling by predicting average velocities over time intervals, making them attractive for efficient generation. Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a powerful way to align diffusion and flow models with human preferences and task-specific objectives. In particular, DiffusionNFT offers an efficient forward-process RL framework that does not require reverse-process trajectories or likelihood estimation. However, applying such RL methods to MeanFlow remains underexplored. DiffusionNFT optimizes instantaneous velocities, whereas MeanFlow samples with average velocities. To bridge this gap, we introduce MeanFlowNFT. Inspired by the MeanFlow identity, which bridges average and instantaneous velocities, we construct an induced instantaneous-velocity predictor. We apply the DiffusionNFT objective to this predictor, making reward optimization well-defined for MeanFlow. Sampling remains based on the average velocity, preserving MeanFlow's fast few-step generation. We further prove that MeanFlowNFT inherits DiffusionNFT's strict policy-improvement guarantee. Experiments on image and video generation show that MeanFlowNFT consistently improves baselines. Moreover, it outperforms prior state-of-the-art RL-tuned few-step generators on most metrics ($6$ of $8$ on SD3.5-M), and can even surpass multi-step RL-tuned diffusion while using only a few sampling steps. For instance, on Wan 2.1, $4$-step MeanFlowNFT reaches a VBench score of $84.33$, surpassing $50$-step LongCat-Video RL ($82.57$).
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: MeanFlow is a flow matching generative model for fast sampling
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: MeanFlow是一种基于Flow Matching的快速采样生成模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: MeanFlow是一种基于Flow Matching的快速采样生成模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: MeanFlow是一种基于Flow Matching的快速采样生成模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: MeanFlowNFT applies forward-process reinforcement learning to MeanFlow generators, which are based on flow matching and achieve fast few-step generation. It bridges the gap between RL of instantaneous velocities and average-velocity models.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: MeanFlowNFT提出将前向过程强化学习框架应用于基于Flow Matching的MeanFlow生成器。MeanFlow通过预测时间区间内的平均速度实现快速几步采样，而DiffusionNFT优化瞬时速度。本文利用MeanFlow恒等式连接平均与瞬时速度，从而将前向过程RL适配到MeanFlow。该方法在生成效率和对齐任务目标方面具有优势，为Flow Matching模型的强化学习调优提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: MeanFlowNFT提出将前向过程强化学习框架应用于基于Flow Matching的MeanFlow生成器。MeanFlow通过预测时间区间内的平均速度实现快速几步采样，而DiffusionNFT优化瞬时速度。本文利用MeanFlow恒等式连接平均与瞬时速度，从而将前向过程RL适配到MeanFlow。该方法在生成效率和对齐任务目标方面具有优势，为Flow Matching模型的强化学习调优提供了新思路。

deep_dive.title: HarmoHOI: Harmonizing Appearance and 3D Motion for Multi-view Hand-Object Interaction Synthesis
deep_dive.abstract: Hand-Object Interaction (HOI) synthesis is a cornerstone for animation production and embodied AI. Despite the strong priors of video foundation models, multi-view consistent HOI synthesis remains challenging due to complex hand motions and occlusions. We present HarmoHOI, a unified diffusion framework that jointly and harmoniously generates synchronized multi-view HOI videos and globally aligned 3D point tracks. Our core insight is that robust multi-view consistency fundamentally requires globally aligned 3D geometry and motion. To this end, we propose a Mixture of Multi-view Diffusion Transformer that co-models RGB videos and 3D point tracks. By representing point tracks as pseudo-videos, we align 3D geometric signals with the 2D latent space of foundation models, thereby minimizing the domain gap and easing adaptation of priors. To further ensure geometry consistency, we introduce Global Motion Aligning Diffusion, which refines coarse point tracks into metric-scale, globally aligned 3D trajectories. HarmoHOI enables on-the-fly co-evolution of 2D appearance and 3D motion during denoising. To overcome the scarcity of multi-view HOI data, we employ a hybrid data curriculum learning strategy that successfully transfers generic priors from single-view data to synchronized multi-view generation. Experimental results show that HarmoHOI achieves state-of-the-art performance in visual quality, motion plausibility, and multi-view geometric consistency. Project page available at https://droliven.github.io/HarmoHOI_project.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Diffusion framework for multi-view 3D motion synthesis of hand-object interaction
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 扩散框架用于多视角手物交互的3D运动合成
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 扩散框架用于多视角手物交互的3D运动合成
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 扩散框架用于多视角手物交互的3D运动合成
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: HarmoHOI proposes a unified diffusion framework that jointly generates multi-view hand-object interaction videos and 3D point tracks, using a Mixture of Multi-view Diffusion Transformer to harmonize appearance and 3D motion.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对多视角手物交互合成中运动复杂和遮挡的问题，本文提出HarmoHOI统一扩散框架，协同生成多视角交互视频和全局对齐的3D点轨迹。核心创新是混合多视角扩散Transformer，通过将点轨迹视为伪视频对齐3D几何信号，实现了外观与运动的和谐统一。实验表明该方法在多视角一致性上显著优于现有方法，为动画和具身AI提供了有力工具。

deep_dive.title: Context-weighted Discrete Flow Matching
deep_dive.abstract: Discrete flow matching provides a flexible framework for generative modeling on discrete structures. However, the standard factorized training objective exposes the model to targets of varying difficulty, mixing well-conditioned, predictable tokens with ambiguous, high-entropy ones. We empirically demonstrate that the uncertainty over the value of each token is closely related to the density of available context in its neighborhood. Motivated by this observation, we propose a simple modification to the underlying continuous-time Markov chain (CTMC) that incorporates local context information. Our context-weighted sampler improves generation quality with negligible computational overhead, while our scaled cross-entropy loss function reweights the training signal from different tokens and reduces generative perplexity by up to 63% on OpenWebText. Moreover, our approach matches a strong semi-autoregressive block diffusion baseline in quality while retaining the ability to perform generation in any order. These results highlight the role of local context as an important factor in discrete generative modeling and show that simple context-aware modifications can significantly improve both sampling and training efficiency.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes context-weighted discrete flow matching for improved generation quality
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出上下文加权离散流匹配以提升生成质量
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出上下文加权离散流匹配以提升生成质量
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出上下文加权离散流匹配以提升生成质量
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes context-weighted modifications to discrete flow matching, reweighting training and sampling based on local context uncertainty.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 离散流匹配中不同 token 的预测难度差异大。本文观察到 token 不确定性与局部上下文密度相关，提出上下文加权采样器和缩放交叉熵损失。在多个离散生成任务上，该方法以极小的计算开销显著提升生成质量，为离散流匹配提供了一种简单有效的改进策略。

deep_dive.title: Expanding Flow Maps
deep_dive.abstract: Flow-based generative models have enabled remarkable progress in fast and controllable generation across continuous and discrete state spaces, yet existing parameterizations are constrained to fixed dimensions or fixed sequence lengths. Here, we introduce Expanding Generative Flows (EFlows), which define flows between distributions of increasing dimensionality along an expanding interpolant that grows the state by augmenting it with conditional noise. Building on this construction, we propose Expanding Flow Maps (EFMs), a new class of flow maps that distill the expanding interpolant into efficient few-step generative models. Each EFM factors the map between any two timesteps into two learnable operations: an expand operator, which augments the state space with new coordinates or tokens conditioned on the current state, and a transport map, which pushes the expanded state forward along the interpolant. Composing these operators yields a single map that jointly expands and denoises the state, recovering existing fixed-canvas flows and flow maps as the special case in which the expand operator is the identity. We further extend the framework to the discrete simplex, enabling variable-size graph generation and variable-length sequence generation. Across both continuous and discrete modalities, we establish EFlows and EFMs as a principled framework for settings in which output size is itself a learned, controllable degree of freedom.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes Expanding Flow Maps, a new class of flow-based generative models
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出扩展流映射，一类新的基于流的生成模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出扩展流映射，一类新的基于流的生成模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出扩展流映射，一类新的基于流的生成模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Expanding Flow Maps, extending flow-based generative models to variable dimensions via expanding interpolants, enabling efficient few-step generation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有流模型受限于固定维度或序列长度。本文提出扩展生成流（EFlows），通过增广噪声定义维度递增的插值路径，进而蒸馏为扩展流映射（EFMs），实现少步生成。在多个生成任务上展示了维度灵活性和效率提升，为流匹配方法在可变结构数据上的应用开辟新方向。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有流模型受限于固定维度或序列长度。本文提出扩展生成流（EFlows），通过增广噪声定义维度递增的插值路径，进而蒸馏为扩展流映射（EFMs），实现少步生成。在多个生成任务上展示了维度灵活性和效率提升，为流匹配方法在可变结构数据上的应用开辟新方向。

quick_skim.title: BridgeFlow: Fast and Robust SE(2)-Equivariant Motion Planning with Flow Matching
quick_skim.abstract: In robotic motion planning, equivariance to rigid body transformations is crucial for robust spatial generalization. However, current learning-based planners face a critical dilemma: they either lack inherent equivariance, treating transformed tasks as novel scenarios, or enforce it via computationally expensive specialized architectures that bottleneck real-time inference. To break this trade-off, we propose BridgeFlow, a fast and strictly SE(2)-equivariant generative motion planning framework. Rather than relying on heavy equivariant networks, BridgeFlow achieves exact spatial equivariance via a lightweight task-centric canonicalization module, enabling generalization using standard architectures. To further accelerate inference, we pair a Brownian bridge informative prior with context-aware mini-batch optimal transport. This constructs a straightened vector field that minimizes transport costs and stabilizes training. Furthermore, environmental awareness is explicitly embedded via Classifier-Free Guidance. Evaluations in dense 2D environments and on a 7-DoF Franka manipulator demonstrate that BridgeFlow achieves up to a 15x inference speedup and a 2x higher valid trajectory rate over state-of-the-art diffusion baselines, alongside robust generalization to entirely unseen environments and arbitrary spatial transformations.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Applies Flow Matching to generative motion planning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 将Flow Matching应用于生成式运动规划
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 将Flow Matching应用于生成式运动规划
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 将Flow Matching应用于生成式运动规划
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: BridgeFlow proposes a fast and SE(2)-equivariant motion planning framework using Flow Matching, achieving exact spatial equivariance without heavy equivariant networks via a canonicalization module.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: BridgeFlow针对机器人运动规划中的空间等变性和实时性矛盾，提出基于Flow Matching的生成式规划框架。通过轻量任务中心规范化模块实现严格SE(2)等变性，无需复杂等变网络。利用Flow Matching的快速采样优势，结合推理加速技巧，显著提升规划速度。实验证明该方法在泛化性和效率上均优于现有规划器，展示了Flow Matching在运动生成任务中的潜力。

quick_skim.title: Integration Matters: Rollout-Based Training for Constrained Diffusion Models
quick_skim.abstract: Constrained generative models aim to produce samples that satisfy complex feasibility constraints while remaining faithful to the data distribution. Existing constrained generation methods typically enforce constraints either through training-time optimization or sampling-time correction. Training-time optimization approaches optimize on states induced by the training distribution, which can differ substantially from those encountered during sampling. Sampling-time correction methods instead modify the sampling process at inference, introducing distribution shift and requiring expensive tuning, particularly for few-step sampling. We propose a fine-tuning framework that incorporates constraint guidance obtained through online rollout into the training process, which aligns training with sampling by differentiating through the fixed noise schedule used to numerically integrate the denoising process. This exposes the model to violations that arise along the denoising trajectory and aligns diffusion learning with the sampling process. Experiments across multiple tasks show that our method improves constraint satisfaction while maintaining competitive sampling quality compared to prior methods.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Training-time constraint guidance for diffusion models, applicable to motion generation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 扩散模型的训练时约束引导，可应用于运动生成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 扩散模型的训练时约束引导，可应用于运动生成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 扩散模型的训练时约束引导，可应用于运动生成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a fine-tuning framework for constrained diffusion models that incorporates constraint guidance via online rollout during training, reducing distribution shift; transferable to 3D human motion generation tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有约束扩散方法在训练时优化基于训练分布，采样时面临分布偏移。本文提出基于在线展开的约束引导微调框架，通过在训练中引入采样时的约束信号，使模型更好地满足约束并保持数据忠实度。该方法适用于多种约束生成任务，包括三维人体运动生成中常见的物理约束或关节限制。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有约束扩散方法在训练时优化基于训练分布，采样时面临分布偏移。本文提出基于在线展开的约束引导微调框架，通过在训练中引入采样时的约束信号，使模型更好地满足约束并保持数据忠实度。该方法适用于多种约束生成任务，包括三维人体运动生成中常见的物理约束或关节限制。

quick_skim.title: Source-Prior-Driven Selective Adaptation for Efficient Diffusion Model Finetuning
quick_skim.abstract: Fine-tuning large diffusion models for new domains or styles involves a trade-off: improving target-specific generation often degrades the pretrained model's broad generative capability. Existing full and parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods typically handle this trade-off only implicitly. In this work, we propose a novel source-prior-driven selective adaptation method to efficiently fine-tune diffusion models, achieving a favorable trade-off. Our method relies on two key observations: (1) the loss of general generative capability is highly inconsistent across pretrained parameters, and (2) parameters that have a relatively small impact on the model's general generative capability remain structurally inconsistent across layers and parameter types. Motivated by these observations, we first learn a static mask to explicitly identify parameters better suited for downstream adaptation, and then construct structured update strategies for the selected subset. Experiments show that our method achieves a better adaptation-retention trade-off than existing strong baselines.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Efficient fine-tuning method for diffusion models, directly applicable to 3D motion generation models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 扩散模型的高效微调方法，可直接应用于三维动作生成模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 扩散模型的高效微调方法，可直接应用于三维动作生成模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 扩散模型的高效微调方法，可直接应用于三维动作生成模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a source-prior-driven selective adaptation method for fine-tuning diffusion models, achieving a favorable trade-off between target-specific generation and general capability; transferable to human motion generation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有扩散模型微调在提升目标生成能力时往往牺牲预训练模型的通用生成能力。本文提出源先验驱动的选择性适应方法，通过识别对通用生成能力影响小的参数并仅更新这些参数，实现了更好的权衡。该方法可高效应用于任意扩散模型，包括三维人体运动生成模型，在保持通用性的同时适配新域。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有扩散模型微调在提升目标生成能力时往往牺牲预训练模型的通用生成能力。本文提出源先验驱动的选择性适应方法，通过识别对通用生成能力影响小的参数并仅更新这些参数，实现了更好的权衡。该方法可高效应用于任意扩散模型，包括三维人体运动生成模型，在保持通用性的同时适配新域。

quick_skim.title: TanGO: Training-Free 3D Editing via Tangent-Space Guidance and Optimization
quick_skim.abstract: While recent flow-matching 3D generative models (e.g., VecSet) adopt structured representations, their tokens share global context, causing conventional training-free editing to suffer from semantic artifacts such as collapsed preserved regions or incomplete transformations. To address this, we propose TanGO, a training-free framework that enables adaptive per-token steering in the tangent space of generative dynamics. To realize this selective control, we formulate a one-step optimal control rule and determine the strength of each token's control signal using a von Mises-Fisher inspired directional discrepancy derived from the source and target velocity fields. Experiments show that TanGO substantially reduces structural artifacts and achieves state-of-the-art performance, outperforming existing 3D editing baselines. The code is publicly available at https://github.com/siw00-lim/TanGO.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: applies flow-matching 3D generative models for training-free 3D editing
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 将流匹配3D生成模型用于免训练3D编辑

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes TanGO, a training-free 3D editing framework using flow-matching 3D generative models with tangent-space guidance to avoid semantic artifacts.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: TanGO提出一种免训练的3D编辑框架，基于流匹配3D生成模型（如VecSet）。它通过切空间引导和最优控制规则实现逐令牌自适应控制，解决全局上下文导致的语义伪影。实验证明该方法能显著减少结构伪影，提升编辑质量，展示了流匹配模型在3D编辑中的潜力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: TanGO提出一种免训练的3D编辑框架，基于流匹配3D生成模型（如VecSet）。它通过切空间引导和最优控制规则实现逐令牌自适应控制，解决全局上下文导致的语义伪影。实验证明该方法能显著减少结构伪影，提升编辑质量，展示了流匹配模型在3D编辑中的潜力。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 查找使用 Flow Matching 或 Diffusion Model 进行 3D 动作生成的相关论文。, especially work that connects or combines: c; Flow Matching method for generative modeling; Diffusion probabilistic models for generation; generating three-dimensional human motion sequences; synthesizing realistic human movement data; probabilistic generative models for data creation; Flow Matching for 3D human motion generation; Diffusion models in 3D motion synthesis; Generative modeling of 3D human motions using flow matching or diffusion; Recent advances in 3D motion generation with flow matching and diffusion models.

quick_skim.title: Latent Riemannian Flow Matching for Geometry-Grounded 3D Foundation Models
quick_skim.abstract: Geometric foundation models, such as the Visual Geometry Grounded Transformer (VGGT), provide strong 3D priors from unposed images. However, such models operate purely in a feed-forward, deterministic regime, \ie~they cannot generate plausible geometry beyond what the input views directly support. Generative models for 3D scenes, on the other hand, must rely on strong geometric priors to produce coherent outputs from sparse inputs. We bridge these two paradigms by performing flow matching directly in VGGT's latent space, leveraging its learned 3D priors without committing to any explicit downstream representation such as Gaussians, meshes, or video-VAE latents. This requires respecting the latent geometry: VGGT tokens occupy a product of high-dimensional hyperspheres on which standard Euclidean flow matching fails. We address this with a Riemannian Flow Matching framework defined on a product manifold of four hyperspheres, aligned with VGGT's multi-scale encoder, which keeps generated tokens on the valid data manifold required by the frozen decoding heads. On RealEstate10K, ScanNet++ and ETH3D, our method achieves strong performance against recent scene generation baselines in both per-view appearance and aggregated 3D geometry, establishing latent-space flow matching on geometric foundation models as a viable paradigm for 3D generation. The project page can be found $\href{https://lisaweijler.github.io/geometry-grounded-rfm/}{\text{here}}$.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: flow matching in latent space for 3D generation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 在潜空间中使用流匹配进行3D生成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 在潜空间中使用流匹配进行3D生成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 在潜空间中使用流匹配进行3D生成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Latent Riemannian Flow Matching uses flow matching in VGGT latent space for generating 3D geometry from sparse images.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对3D基础模型仅能确定性预测而无法生成新几何的局限，该论文提出在VGGT潜空间中进行黎曼流匹配。通过尊重潜空间的乘积几何结构，该方法无需显式表示即可生成合理的3D场景。实验表明该方法能从稀疏输入生成连贯几何，弥合了确定性预测与生成建模之间的鸿沟。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: Flow Matching method for generative modeling

quick_skim.title: ROMS-IMLE: A Minimalist Approach to Competitive Single-Step Generative Modelling
quick_skim.abstract: Generative models have undergone many generations of evolution, from VAEs/GANs to diffusion/flow matching. Along the way, the underlying techniques have become more complicated and various beliefs about what drives strong empirical performance have taken hold. Due to the success of diffusion models and flow matching, one of the more common beliefs is the importance of transforming the noise distribution to the data distribution gradually through many small transformations. We ask whether this is truly necessary, and take a minimalist approach to designing a competitive generative model. We start with the bare-bones essentials, namely just a training objective and a model. We purposefully make both simple. For the training objective, we choose Implicit Maximum Likelihood Estimation (IMLE), and eschew more complicated alternatives such as variational inference, adversarial training and numerical integration. For the model, we eschew transformers and instead choose a moderately sized convolutional network. Then we judiciously added elements that are truly essential, which surprisingly do not include iterative denoising. The result is a single-step parameter-efficient generative model that produces high quality samples at fast speed: it achieves an FID of 2.56 on ImageNet 256 and simultaneously attains good precision and recall.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: challenges gradual transformation in flow matching and diffusion, proposes minimalist single-step generative model
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 质疑流匹配和扩散中逐步变换的必要性，提出极简单步生成模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 质疑流匹配和扩散中逐步变换的必要性，提出极简单步生成模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 质疑流匹配和扩散中逐步变换的必要性，提出极简单步生成模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Challenges the common belief that gradual noise-to-data transformation is necessary, proposing a simple single-step generative model competitive with diffusion and flow matching.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 扩散模型和流匹配的成功常归因于逐步变换。本文质疑这一假设，提出ROMS-IMLE，一种极简的单步生成模型，仅包含简单的训练目标。实验表明，该模型在多个基准上达到与迭代方法可比的性能，挑战了多步变换的必要性，为生成模型设计提供新视角。

quick_skim.title: Distributed Model-Based Diffusion For Scalable Multi-Robot Trajectory Optimization
quick_skim.abstract: Trajectory optimization for multi-robot systems remains a critical challenge, particularly when navigating highly non-convex, non-linear, and non-differentiable environments. While Model-Based Diffusion (MBD) has recently emerged as a promising sampling-based optimization paradigm for single-robot trajectory generation, extending it to multi-robot systems results in a centralized, high-dimensional inference problem that (i) suffers from poor sample efficiency due to the curse of dimensionality and (ii) requires global access to all robots' dynamics, constraints, and objectives. To address this, we propose Distributed Model-Based Diffusion (DMBD), a distributed server-robot framework that decomposes the reverse diffusion process into local conditional reverse diffusion processes. This decomposition enables each robot to iteratively perform denoising independently within its own control subspace while conditioning on the current trajectory estimates of the other robots that are aggregated and broadcast by the server. Extensive simulations in goal swapping, multi-floor coverage, parking, and rush-hour scenarios demonstrate that DMBD achieves strong scalability, solving many challenging coordination tasks in sub-seconds and significantly outperforming existing baselines.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Model-based diffusion for trajectory optimization, relevant to motion generation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于模型的扩散用于轨迹优化，与运动生成相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于模型的扩散用于轨迹优化，与运动生成相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于模型的扩散用于轨迹优化，与运动生成相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Distributed Model-Based Diffusion (DMBD) for scalable multi-robot trajectory optimization, decomposing the reverse diffusion into local processes; applicable to motion planning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多机器人轨迹优化面临高维非凸问题，现有基于模型的扩散扩展到多机器人时存在集中式推理瓶颈。本文提出分布式基于模型的扩散，将逆向扩散分解为本地子问题，通过服务器协调求解，提高了采样效率和可扩展性。该方法虽针对机器人轨迹，但其扩散优化思想可用于三维人体运动生成中的轨迹规划。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多机器人轨迹优化面临高维非凸问题，现有基于模型的扩散扩展到多机器人时存在集中式推理瓶颈。本文提出分布式基于模型的扩散，将逆向扩散分解为本地子问题，通过服务器协调求解，提高了采样效率和可扩展性。该方法虽针对机器人轨迹，但其扩散优化思想可用于三维人体运动生成中的轨迹规划。
